script:
#!/bin/bash
set +e
echo '========================================'
echo 'PHASE 4: DOCKER PORT HARDENING'
echo '========================================'

echo ''
echo '--- 4.1 Reading all docker-compose files ---'

for f in /opt/n8n/docker-compose.yml /opt/openclaw/docker-compose.yml /opt/clawtest/docker-compose.yml; do
  echo ''
  echo "=== FILE: $f ==="
  if [ -f "$f" ]; then
    cat "$f"
  else
    echo 'NOT FOUND'
  fi
done

echo ''
echo '--- 4.2 WordPress compose (find) ---'
find /opt /root /home -maxdepth 5 -name 'docker-compose*.yml' -path '*mebelit*' 2>/dev/null
find /opt /root /home -maxdepth 5 -name 'docker-compose*.yml' -path '*wordpress*' 2>/dev/null
find /opt /root /home -maxdepth 5 -name 'docker-compose*.yml' -path '*wp*' 2>/dev/null

# Find the WP compose by container name
echo ''
echo '--- 4.3 Finding WP compose via docker inspect ---'
docker inspect mebelit-wp-wordpress-1 --format '{{.Config.Labels}}' 2>/dev/null | tr ',' '\n' | grep 'project.working_dir\|project.config_files'

echo ''
echo '--- 4.4 Port 2053 check ---'
ss -tlnp | grep 2053

echo ''
echo '--- 4.5 What is on ports 3000, 8000, 9090, 3102 ---'
for p in 3000 8000 9090 3102 10050; do
  echo "PORT $p:"
  ss -tlnp | grep ":$p "
  # If it is a known PID, check what it is
  PID=$(ss -tlnp | grep ":$p " | grep -oP 'pid=\K[0-9]+')
  if [ -n "$PID" ]; then
    echo "  Process: $(ps -p $PID -o comm= 2>/dev/null) | CWD: $(readlink /proc/$PID/cwd 2>/dev/null) | CMD: $(cat /proc/$PID/cmdline 2>/dev/null | tr '\0' ' ')"
  fi
  echo ''
done

echo '========================================'
echo 'PHASE 4 RECON COMPLETE'
echo '========================================'
intent: Phase 4: Read docker-compose files and identify all exposed services
